purpose: Idea-wizard no-cargo inventory for CLI contract gates. The report scans tracked harness CLI contract test gates, separates dirty/untracked candidate gates from real recommendations, and suggests the next missing static invariant without adding Rust test files while RCH validation is blocked.

candidate_invariants.recommendation_title: Pin that paired CLI contract gates do not call env::set_var
candidate_invariants.rationale: Environment mutation makes parallel harness gates order-dependent; if a dirty gate already exists, keep it pending rather than recommending a duplicate.

candidate_invariants.recommendation_title: Pin every manifest-to-test pairing through an explicit file contract
candidate_invariants.rationale: The manifest/test pairing surface is easy to duplicate accidentally; dirty pending gates must be surfaced before recommending more Rust tests.

candidate_invariants.recommendation_title: Pin that paired CLI contract gates do not mutate process current_dir
candidate_invariants.rationale: Changing the process working directory inside test gates creates cross-test flake risk without requiring cargo execution to detect.

candidate_invariants.recommendation_title: Pin that paired CLI contract gates do not open network sockets
candidate_invariants.rationale: CLI contract gates should stay deterministic and offline; network-capable gates should be explicit integration tests instead.

candidate_invariants.recommendation_title: Pin that paired CLI contract gates do not issue process-kill commands
candidate_invariants.rationale: Static contract gates must not terminate unrelated processes; this inventory can suggest the invariant without running any compiled tests.

validation_commands: jq -e '(.tracked_gate_count >= 200) and ([.dirty_pending_candidates[].candidate_id] | index("no_env_set_var")) and ([.dirty_pending_candidates[].candidate_id] | index("paired_gate_file_contract")) and ([.recommendations[].candidate_id] | index("no_current_dir_mutation"))' target/conformance/cli_contract_gate_inventory.report.json
cargo_reason: This is a shell/Python JSON inventory over tracked files and git status; it does not touch Rust code or require compiled tests.
